problem.title: Closest Binary Search Tree Value
problem.description: Given the root of a binary search tree and a target value, return the value in the BST that is closest to the target.
assumptions: If two values are equally close, return the smaller one.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Binary Search
solutions.description: Traverse the BST like a binary search: at each node update the closest candidate if the current node is nearer to the target, then move left if the target is smaller or right if larger. The BST property guarantees we only follow the branch that can possibly contain a closer value, giving O(h) time.
